name: Reverse Translate AA to DNA
description: Converts an amino acid sequence into a codon-optimized DNA sequence for a target host organism and saves it as a GenBank file with a proper CDS annotation. A warning is included if the output is under 1000 bp (antiSMASH minimum).

inputs.name: aa_sequence
inputs.description: Amino acid sequence in single-letter code (e.g. 'MKVLSAFG').

inputs.name: host
inputs.description: Target organism for codon optimization. Supported: e_coli, s_coelicolor, s_albus, s_venezuelae, p_putida, s_cerevisiae. Defaults to e_coli.

inputs.name: filename
inputs.description: Output GenBank filename (must end in .gb). Saved to modules/pks/data/. Defaults to synthetic_pks.gb.

outputs.description: Dictionary with: status ('success'), dna_sequence (optimized DNA string), dna_length_bp (integer), file_saved_at (path to the GenBank file), message (summary string), and optionally warning (if dna_length_bp < 1000).

output.message: Sequence optimized for s_coelicolor and saved to module1.gb.
output.warning: Output is only 12 bp. antiSMASH requires a minimum of 1000 bp...

output.message: Sequence optimized for e_coli and saved to test.gb.
